mnemonics.title: Tree Traversal Order
mnemonics.content: Remember traversals with: LRN (Left-Right-Root) = Postorder; NLR (Node-Left-Right) = Preorder; LNR (Left-Node-Right) = Inorder. Think: N=Node processed first in Preorder, R=Root last in Postorder.

mnemonics.title: Binary Tree Properties
mnemonics.content: For binary tree: Max nodes at level L = 2^L. Max nodes in tree of height H = 2^(H+1) - 1. Full tree: Leaves = Internal nodes + 1.

mnemonics.title: BST Inorder Magic
mnemonics.content: Inorder on BST = Sorted Order! Left < Root < Right makes it happen automatically.

acronyms.usage: A binary tree where left subtree values < root value < right subtree values

visualTips.visualization: Think of a family tree or organizational chart. Root at top, children below, like an upside-down tree.
Draw a simple tree and trace your finger: Inorder = go left, visit, go right; Preorder = visit, go left, go right; Postorder = go left, go right, visit.
Complete = fill from left like filling a pyramid; Full = each parent has 0 or 2 kids (no only child); Perfect = all levels full (both complete and full).

analogies.analogy: Like the CEO of a company - the top boss from whom everyone reports.
Like employees with no subordinates - they do the actual work.
Preorder is like introducing yourself before describing details; Postorder is like cleaning your room before leaving; Inorder is like reading a book page by page in order.
Like a dictionary where words are organized: all words before 'M' on left, all after 'M' on right - makes searching fast!
